novelty_flags.label: Conjecture 1
novelty_flags.one_line: The drafter handoff omitted the required named literature checklist; §5 also misses close monotonicity-with-defiers and noncompliance-bounds comparators deChaisemartin2017, DahlHuberMellace2023, and GabrielSachsSjolander2023, which should be explicitly separated from the two-cap ATE frontier.

strengthening_paths.suggestion: Add a proper 4-10 item literature checklist mapping each paper to the relevant §8 result, including RichardsonRobins2010, SwansonHernanMillerRobinsRichardson2018, GabrielSachsSjolander2023, deChaisemartin2017, DahlHuberMellace2023, Noack2021, Yap2022, LevisBonviniZengKeeleKennedy2025, SachsJonzonSjolanderGabriel2023, and DuarteFinkelsteinKnoxMummoloShpitser2024.

For Conjecture 1, state a non-subsumption lemma: even if fixed-defier endpoints B_L(t), B_U(t) are imported from Richardson-Robins, the never-taker cap creates new observable lower-truncation regions and joint-cap active facets; give at least one open-set condition, not just a prose claim.

Add a small nondegenerate numerical observed law where lowering nu_bar strictly moves one endpoint while the defier cap is fixed, and identify the active joint-cap facet family; this would make the flagship strict-shrinkage claim auditable.

Clarify the bounded-continuous Y convention against binary-outcome Balke-Pearl formulas and Levis et al.'s bounded-outcome extension so the no-cap sanity check is not read as relying on a binary-Y theorem.

recommended_next_step: Revise the literature checklist and §5/§8 comparison blocks; the kernel can proceed without pivot once the missed prior-art separation is explicit.
